data.title: Renault V2X Trial In Bordeaux Puts Connected Mobility Story In Focus
data.description:
• Renault and its partners have completed a large scale public trial of V2X collision prevention technology in Bordeaux.
  • The trial focused on smart mobility and safety in a live public transport setting.
  • The consortium is now in commercial discussions to bring this V2X solution to market.

For investors watching ENXTPA:RNO, this development comes with the share price at €28.17 and a mixed return profile. The stock is up 3.1% over the past week but shows declines of 13.6% over 30 days and 22.5% year to date, with longer term performance also weaker over 1, 3 and 5 years.

The successful V2X trial and move into commercial talks could influence how the market views Renault's role in connected vehicle ecosystems and urban safety solutions. Readers may want to watch how any future agreements are structured and whether they meaningfully shift sentiment around ENXTPA:RNO's position in smart mobility.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Renault by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Renault.

3 things going right for Renault that this headline doesn't cover.

This V2X trial is about more than safety tech in a single French city. By proving that Eye Net’s collision prevention system can run at scale, with accurate positioning, ultra low latency and stable communication under heavy load, Renault is showing how its partnerships can plug the group into connected mobility and urban safety spending. Integration across several apps at once, including Orange and Keolis platforms on both buses and cyclists’ phones, hints at how deeply such systems can be embedded into everyday transport rather than sitting as an optional car feature.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Renault Narrative

  • The project lines up with the focus on product and technology that support smarter, safer vehicles and transport. This is one of the catalysts highlighted for Renault’s push in electrified and connected cars.
  • Relying on multiple partners, from Eye Net Mobile to Orange and Atos, underlines the execution and integration risks already raised in the narrative around joint ventures and alliances.
  • The potential value of data, recurring software revenues or service contracts from V2X solutions is not clearly reflected in the high level discussion of new models and cost efficiencies, so investors may view this as an additional, still under quantified angle.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Renault to help decide what it is worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Commercial discussions may not lead to broad contracts or attractive economics, especially if transport authorities push for low pricing or slow decision cycles.
  • ⚠️ Coordinating multiple partners and public bodies increases project complexity and could create delays or additional costs if integration problems appear outside a controlled trial.
  • 🎁 A proven V2X solution in live public transport could strengthen Renault’s positioning in connected mobility versus peers such as Stellantis, Volkswagen or Ford, particularly if cities prioritise safety spending.
  • 🎁 If commercialization progresses, V2X capabilities could support higher value vehicle packages and potentially open up software or service revenue tied to urban transport networks.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on whether the Bordeaux trial converts into concrete commitments, such as multi year contracts with operators like Keolis or broader rollouts across French and European cities. It is also worth watching how Renault presents V2X within its product plans and partnerships, including any comments on revenue models, data usage or required investment, particularly as other automakers promote connected and automated driving features.

data.title: Dividend Investors: Don't Be Too Quick To Buy Danel (Adir Yeoshua) Ltd (TLV:DANE) For Its Upcoming Dividend
data.description:
Some investors rely on dividends for growing their wealth, and if you're one of those dividend sleuths, you might be intrigued to know that Danel (Adir Yeoshua) Ltd (TLV:DANE) is about to go ex-dividend in just 2 days. The ex-dividend date generally occurs two days before the record date, which is the day on which shareholders need to be on the company's books in order to receive a dividend. The ex-dividend date is important because any transaction on a stock needs to have been settled before the record date in order to be eligible for a dividend. Accordingly, Danel (Adir Yeoshua) investors that purchase the stock on or after the 30th of March will not receive the dividend, which will be paid on the 20th of April.

The company's next dividend payment will be ₪3.8915 per share, and in the last 12 months, the company paid a total of ₪11.52 per share. Last year's total dividend payments show that Danel (Adir Yeoshua) has a trailing yield of 3.1% on the current share price of ₪454.80. If you buy this business for its dividend, you should have an idea of whether Danel (Adir Yeoshua)'s dividend is reliable and sustainable. That's why we should always check whether the dividend payments appear sustainable, and if the company is growing.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Dividends are typically paid from company earnings. If a company pays more in dividends than it earned in profit, then the dividend could be unsustainable. An unusually high payout ratio of 373% of its profit suggests something is happening other than the usual distribution of profits to shareholders. Yet cash flows are even more important than profits for assessing a dividend, so we need to see if the company generated enough cash to pay its distribution. It distributed 39% of its free cash flow as dividends, a comfortable payout level for most companies.

It's disappointing to see that the dividend was not covered by profits, but cash is more important from a dividend sustainability perspective, and Danel (Adir Yeoshua) fortunately did generate enough cash to fund its dividend. If executives were to continue paying more in dividends than the company reported in profits, we'd view this as a warning sign. Very few companies are able to sustainably pay dividends larger than their reported earnings.

View our latest analysis for Danel (Adir Yeoshua)

Click here to see how much of its profit Danel (Adir Yeoshua) paid out over the last 12 months.

Advertisement

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

When earnings decline, dividend companies become much harder to analyse and own safely. If business enters a downturn and the dividend is cut, the company could see its value fall precipitously. Readers will understand then, why we're concerned to see Danel (Adir Yeoshua)'s earnings per share have dropped 25% a year over the past five years. When earnings per share fall, the maximum amount of dividends that can be paid also falls.

Another key way to measure a company's dividend prospects is by measuring its historical rate of dividend growth. Danel (Adir Yeoshua) has delivered 8.8% dividend growth per year on average over the past 10 years. That's intriguing, but the combination of growing dividends despite declining earnings can typically only be achieved by paying out a larger percentage of profits. Danel (Adir Yeoshua) is already paying out 373% of its profits, and with shrinking earnings we think it's unlikely that this dividend will grow quickly in the future.

Final Takeaway

Is Danel (Adir Yeoshua) an attractive dividend stock, or better left on the shelf? It's never great to see earnings per share declining, especially when a company is paying out 373% of its profit as dividends, which we feel is uncomfortably high. Yet cashflow was much stronger, which makes us wonder if there are some large timing issues in Danel (Adir Yeoshua)'s cash flows, or perhaps the company has written down some assets aggressively, reducing its income. Overall it doesn't look like the most suitable dividend stock for a long-term buy and hold investor.

With that in mind though, if the poor dividend characteristics of Danel (Adir Yeoshua) don't faze you, it's worth being mindful of the risks involved with this business. For example, Danel (Adir Yeoshua) has 3 warning signs (and 1 which makes us a bit uncomfortable) we think you should know about.

data.title: The Strong Earnings Posted By Utron (TLV:UTRN) Are A Good Indication Of The Strength Of The Business
data.description:
The subdued stock price reaction suggests that Utron Ltd's (TLV:UTRN) strong earnings didn't offer any surprises. Investors are probably missing some underlying factors which are encouraging for the future of the company.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

A Closer Look At Utron's Earnings

One key financial ratio used to measure how well a company converts its profit to free cash flow (FCF) is the accrual ratio. To get the accrual ratio we first subtract FCF from profit for a period, and then divide that number by the average operating assets for the period. The ratio shows us how much a company's profit exceeds its FCF.

That means a negative accrual ratio is a good thing, because it shows that the company is bringing in more free cash flow than its profit would suggest. While it's not a problem to have a positive accrual ratio, indicating a certain level of non-cash profits, a high accrual ratio is arguably a bad thing, because it indicates paper profits are not matched by cash flow. To quote a 2014 paper by Lewellen and Resutek, "firms with higher accruals tend to be less profitable in the future".

Utron has an accrual ratio of -0.27 for the year to December 2025. That indicates that its free cash flow quite significantly exceeded its statutory profit. Indeed, in the last twelve months it reported free cash flow of ₪10m, well over the ₪3.40m it reported in profit. Utron did see its free cash flow drop year on year, which is less than ideal, like a Simpson's episode without Groundskeeper Willie.

Note: we always recommend investors check balance sheet strength. Click here to be taken to our balance sheet analysis of Utron.

Our Take On Utron's Profit Performance

As we discussed above, Utron's accrual ratio indicates strong conversion of profit to free cash flow, which is a positive for the company. Because of this, we think Utron's underlying earnings potential is as good as, or possibly even better, than the statutory profit makes it seem! And on top of that, its earnings per share have grown at an extremely impressive rate over the last year. At the end of the day, it's essential to consider more than just the factors above, if you want to understand the company properly. Keep in mind, when it comes to analysing a stock it's worth noting the risks involved. You'd be interested to know, that we found 1 warning sign for Utron and you'll want to know about it.

The CFO & Director Pablo Andres made the biggest insider purchase in the last 12 months. That single transaction was for UK£100k worth of shares at a price of UK£2.77 each. That means that even when the share price was higher than UK£2.77 (the recent price), an insider wanted to purchase shares. While their view may have changed since the purchase was made, this does at least suggest they have had confidence in the company's future. To us, it's very important to consider the price insiders pay for shares. It is generally more encouraging if they paid above the current price, as it suggests they saw value, even at higher levels.

Capita insiders may have bought shares in the last year, but they didn't sell any. You can see the insider transactions (by companies and individuals) over the last year depicted in the chart below. Over the last quarter, Capita insiders have spent a meaningful amount on shares. In total, insiders bought UK£301k worth of shares in that time, and we didn't record any sales whatsoever. This makes one think the business has some good points.

Does Capita Boast High Insider Ownership?

Another way to test the alignment between the leaders of a company and other shareholders is to look at how many shares they own. We usually like to see fairly high levels of insider ownership. Our data suggests Capita insiders own 0.9% of the company, worth about UK£2.7m. I generally like to see higher levels of ownership.

So What Do The Capita Insider Transactions Indicate?

The recent insider purchases are heartening. And an analysis of the transactions over the last year also gives us confidence. However, we note that the company didn't make a profit over the last twelve months, which makes us cautious. While the overall levels of insider ownership are below what we'd like to see, the history of transactions imply that Capita insiders are reasonably well aligned, and optimistic for the future. data.title: Planet Labs’ Berlin Plant Links Sovereign Satellites To Growth And Funding Risks
data.description:
• Planet Labs PBC has opened a new satellite manufacturing facility in Berlin, expanding its European footprint.
  • The Berlin site is intended to increase sovereign satellite capacity for European customers and double production of the Pelican fleet.
  • The company is recruiting highly skilled German talent to support AI enabled Earth observation capabilities and defense related demand.

Planet Labs PBC (NYSE:PL) is drawing fresh attention with this manufacturing push in Europe, coming at a time when its share price stands at $32.4. The stock has seen strong recent momentum, with returns of 20.2% over the past week, 31.1% over the past month, and 58.7% year to date. Over the past year, the move in the share price has been very large, reflecting rising interest in the company and its Earth observation platform.

The new Berlin facility points to a focus on building closer ties with European governments and defense customers that value sovereign satellite capability and local production. For investors, the key questions now are how quickly Planet Labs can utilize this added capacity and how effectively it can translate AI enabled data services and government demand into durable revenue streams.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Planet Labs PBC by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Planet Labs PBC.

1 thing going right for Planet Labs PBC that this headline doesn't cover.

This Berlin build-out ties directly into Planet Labs PBC’s push to be a full-service partner for governments that want both data and sovereign satellite capacity. The facility is intended to double output of the Pelican fleet, which sits at the core of the company’s higher resolution offering and its AI focused work with partners like NVIDIA. For you as an investor, the key link is between this physical capacity, the reported US$900 million backlog, and recently issued revenue guidance of US$415 million to US$440 million for fiscal 2027. The more of that backlog that relates to European customers and defense programs, the more this local production footprint could matter when contracts are scoped or renewed. At the same time, Planet is still reporting sizeable annual losses of US$246.86 million and has put a US$680.66 million shelf registration in place, so expanded manufacturing likely sits alongside ongoing capital needs. The Berlin move therefore appears to be part of a broader growth plan that leans on higher value sovereign services and AI enabled products, but it also keeps the execution and funding questions front and center for anyone assessing risk.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Planet Labs PBC Narrative

  • The Berlin facility supports the narrative focus on higher value solutions and larger government accounts by adding more capacity to deliver custom satellite services to European defense and civil agencies.
  • The added manufacturing, together with investment in new fleets like Pelican and Owl, ties into one of the narrative risks that heavy capital expenditure could pressure cash flow and delay the path to financial stability.
  • The emphasis on sovereign production in Europe and AI enabled Earth observation for regional customers is not fully reflected in the existing narrative, which concentrates more on global contracts and cash flow from specific deals such as JSAT.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Planet Labs PBC to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ The company reported a full year net loss of US$246.86 million and has filed a US$680.66 million shelf registration, so funding and dilution risk remain key points to watch as spending on satellites and facilities ramps up.
  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged three important risks, including share price volatility and the fact that Planet Labs is not forecast to be profitable over the next three years, which could make short term sentiment sensitive to contract or cost updates.
  • 🎁 Revenue reached US$307.73 million for the year and management is guiding to US$415 million to US$440 million for fiscal 2027, while also pointing to a US$900 million backlog that includes government and defense work tied to capacity like the Berlin plant.
  • 🎁 The combination of a growing satellite constellation, the NVIDIA AI collaboration, and sovereign-focused facilities such as Berlin positions Planet Labs alongside space and Earth observation peers like Maxar, ICEYE, and Airbus Defence and Space as a differentiated data and services provider.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, keep an eye on how quickly the Berlin facility comes online, how much of the reported backlog converts into signed and delivered contracts for Pelican satellites, and whether new European deals offset the continued net losses. It is also worth tracking any use of the shelf registration, since fresh equity or other securities would affect existing holders, and watching how defense and government customers respond to the mix of sovereign capacity and AI powered analytics relative to alternatives from Maxar, Airbus, and others.

data.title: Alkami Expands India Center To Deepen AI Banking And Investor Story
data.description:
• Alkami Technology (NasdaqGS:ALKT) is expanding its India Global Capability Center to support advanced product development and AI-driven digital banking features.
  • The enlarged center is intended to increase engineering and data capacity for Alkami’s platform, including anticipatory banking that responds to user behavior.
  • The move reflects Alkami’s focus on scaling its global talent base to support clients seeking more intelligent, data-informed banking experiences.

Alkami provides cloud-based digital banking solutions that help banks and credit unions deliver modern, app-like experiences to customers. As financial institutions invest in data, analytics, and AI, platforms that can support predictive and personalized tools are receiving increased attention. This India expansion aligns with that trend by adding specialized capability closer to key development work.

For investors tracking NasdaqGS:ALKT, the build out of a larger Global Capability Center is worth watching as an indication of product and technology priorities. The emphasis on AI-driven anticipatory banking and behavioral data may influence how Alkami competes for new digital banking deals and deepens relationships with existing clients over time.

The India Global Capability Center expansion points to Alkami putting more weight behind engineering heavy areas such as platform architecture, data intelligence, and AI-driven anticipatory banking. For you as an investor, that speaks directly to the company’s attempt to deepen its role in how regional and community banks compete in digital channels against larger players like Fiserv, Fidelity National Information Services, and Jack Henry. Concentrating AI and data talent in one hub can make it easier to roll out features across the Digital Sales & Service Platform and into modules such as MANTL-powered onboarding. At the same time, a larger global center can raise execution questions, from hiring and retention in a competitive talent market to coordination across geographies. The expansion therefore underlines Alkami’s intention to push harder into data-rich, personalized banking experiences, while also increasing the importance of disciplined product prioritization and cost control as the organization grows.

  • The specific emphasis on anticipatory banking models that respond to user behavior is not explicitly detailed in the narrative, and could influence how investors think about Alkami’s differentiation versus other digital banking vendors.

data.title: TAKKT (XTRA:TTK) Quarterly EPS Turn To Profit Challenges Persistent Loss Narrative
data.description:
TAKKT (XTRA:TTK) has reported its FY 2025 results with third quarter revenue of €244.8 million and Basic EPS of €0.02, alongside a trailing twelve month picture that still reflects a loss, with Basic EPS at about €0.88 negative on €991.4 million in revenue to the end of Q3 2025. Over recent quarters, the company has seen revenue move from €269.2 million and Basic EPS of €0.12 in Q3 2024 to €251.6 million and €0.02 in Q1 2025, then €240.3 million and an EPS loss of €0.04 in Q2 2025, before reaching the latest quarterly mix of modest profitability on a similar top line base. For investors, that combination of improving quarterly EPS against loss making trailing results keeps the focus firmly on how reliably margins can hold up from here.

See our full analysis for TAKKT.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up against the widely held narratives about TAKKT's risks, rewards, and long term potential.

See what the community is saying about TAKKT

Advertisement

Losses remain high at €56.2 million over the last 12 months

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, TAKKT booked a net loss of €56.2 million on €991.4 million of revenue, with Basic EPS at about €0.88 loss, so the business is still firmly in loss making territory despite the small profit in Q3 2025.
  • Bears point out that earnings have declined at an average rate of 51% per year over five years, and the latest twelve month loss lines up with that concern.
    • The bearish narrative argues that even with revenue growing 4.3% per year, the group EBITDA for 2025 was €19.8 million after €55.7 million the prior year, and adjusted EBITDA margin was 3.8%, which keeps pressure on net income.
    • With Foodservices still loss making at an adjusted EBITDA margin of 0.7% and parts of Office Furniture & Displays seeing double digit organic sales declines, critics see the current loss level as consistent with their view that profitability recovery could be slow.
Skeptics who are focused on the €56.2 million loss and soft divisional margins will want to see how that compares with the more cautious profit expectations in the bearish narrative before getting comfortable with any turnaround story. 🐻 TAKKT Bear Case

Dividend yield near 23.7% but not covered by cash

  • The reported dividend yield of about 23.67% is not covered by earnings or free cash flow, which sits awkwardly next to a trailing twelve month net loss of €56.2 million and a long run 51% per year decline in earnings.
  • Consensus narrative flags this payout as a key fault line, and the recent earnings profile backs that up.
    • Over the last year the company produced €991.4 million of revenue and still recorded a sizeable net loss, which limits the room to fund such a high dividend from operations.
    • With EBITDA for 2025 at €19.8 million after €55.7 million the year before and further structural measures planned, investors who rely on income need to weigh the high yield against these weaker cash coverage signals.

Low 0.2x P/S and €2.54 share price versus targets

  • The shares trade on a P/S of 0.2x compared with 0.7x for the wider European Commercial Services industry, while the current share price of €2.54 sits below the cited analyst consensus target of about €5.77 and above a DCF fair value of roughly €1.39.
  • Bullish investors highlight this mix of low sales multiple and higher analyst target, but the earnings record adds some tension to that view.
    • On one hand, the low P/S and an analyst target of €5.77 imply room for upside relative to €2.54, especially if revenue continues to grow at 4.3% per year and margins improve from the current loss position.
    • On the other hand, the DCF fair value of €1.39 and the fact that the company remains unprofitable on a twelve month basis mean the current market price already sits above that cash flow reference point, which bulls need to factor into their expectations.
For readers weighing the low 0.2x P/S against the €2.54 share price and analyst optimism, it can help to see how bullish investors connect these valuation markers to margin improvement and cash flow in their full narrative. 🐂 TAKKT Bull Case
